answer: The Amazon River is 6,992 km long, surpassing the Nile River's 6,852 km. Recent studies confirm the Amazon's length is longer than previously thought. The Amazon is now considered the world's longest river.

results.title: Expedition Wants to Prove the Amazon is the World's Longest River » Explorersweb
results.snippet: The conventional source of the Amazon is the Apurimac River in southern Peru. The Amazon starts from several proposed sources high in the Andes, including the Apurimac River. In April 2024, he will mount an expedition from Apacheta Creek to the mouth of the river, highlighting the Amazon’s superior length to the Nile. Though many have traversed the Amazon River before, his expedition will be the first to do so in solar-powered boats. Some 640km north of Apacheta Creek, this adds about 80km to the Amazon’s length instead of the 593km if the Apacheta Creek source is used as the starting point. The Contos source would make the Amazon shorter than the Nile. Sanada will use both proposed sources of the Amazon as starting points. Applying each of these to the Amazon makes a difference of 6,510km (Contos’ ephemeral Mantaro source) to 6,993km (the perennial Apacheta Creek source). Next spring, Sanada’s team will spend seven months mapping and measuring the river from both the Apacheta Creek and Contos’ source.
